task: I’ve got a couple of moving parts on a job this week and need to keep the whole thing from drifting. What’s the best way to lay out the next few days so crews, deliveries, and inspections stay lined up without wasting time?

turns.agent_response:
A good way to keep it from drifting is to build a **simple, dependency-based 3–5 day lookahead** and run it like a control sheet, not just a calendar.

### 1) Start with the critical chain
List the items that must happen in order:
- **Prereqs**: permits, materials, access, equipment, utilities shutoffs
- **Crew tasks**: who needs to do what, and in what sequence
- **Inspections / signoffs**: what has to be passed before the next step
- **Deliveries**: what must arrive before crews can start

If one item slips, mark what it affects immediately.

### 2) Build the schedule around “must-start” times
For each day, lay it out like this:
- **Morning**: deliveries, setup, inspections, high-priority crew work
- **Midday**: production tasks that depend on the morning being complete
- **End of day**: cleanup, next-day staging, confirmation calls

Put the hard constraints first:
- inspection windows
- delivery windows
- crew arrival times
- access restrictions

### 3) Use a one-page daily coordination sheet
For each day, track:
- **Task**
- **Owner**
- **Start time**
- **Dependency**
- **Status**
- **Risk / backup plan**

Example:
- Delivery of framing lumber — Supplier — 7:00–9:00 AM — must arrive before framing crew starts — confirmed — backup: alternate supplier
- Rough inspection — Inspector — 10:30 AM — framing complete — scheduled — backup: hold crew on punch list
- Framing crew — Crew A — 8:00 AM — materials on site — pending delivery — backup: prep layout and staging

### 4) Add buffers where drift usually happens
Protect the plan with:
- **30–60 min buffer** before inspections
- **same-day contingency work** if a delivery is late
- **one “float” task** per crew so nobody sits idle
- **end-of-day check-in** to reset the next day

### 5) Run a short daily coordination call
Keep it to 10–15 minutes:
- What got done yesterday?
- What must happen today?
- What could block us?
- Who needs to be notified now?

### 6) Keep one person owning the board
Assign one coordinator to:
- confirm deliveries
- verify inspection times
- update crews on changes
- escalate issues fast

### Simple rule of thumb
If a task has a dependency, don’t schedule it as “sometime that day.” Give it a **specific time window** and a **backup plan**.

If you want, I can turn this into a **fillable 3-day job coordination template** you can use for crews, deliveries, and inspections.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but it’s still a little generic. I need something I can actually run with on a live job.

Lay it out as a 3-day control sheet with:
- exact time blocks
- who owns each block
- what gets checked before the next crew moves
- where the backup work goes if an inspection or delivery slips

And make it look like a site foreman would use it, not some office calendar. A simple template with morning / midday / end-of-day, plus a line for risks and hold points, is the kind of thing I need.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **site-foreman style 3-day control sheet** you can actually run with. It’s built to keep crews, deliveries, and inspections from stepping on each other.

---

# 3-Day Job Control Sheet
**Job:** ____________________  **Location:** ____________________  
**Foreman:** __________________  **Date:** ____________________  
**Weather / Access Notes:** _____________________________________

## Rules for the 3 days
- **Nothing moves forward until the hold point is cleared**
- **If a delivery slips, crew switches to backup work**
- **If an inspection slips, crew stays on prep / punch / staging**
- **End of day = confirm next day’s first move**

---

## DAY 1 — SETUP / FIRST PUSH

| Time Block | Crew / Owner | Work to Run | Check Before Next Crew Moves | Backup Work if Delayed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **6:30–7:00 AM** | Foreman + Leads | Roll call, safety, job walk, confirm deliveries/inspection windows | Access open, tools on site, materials confirmed | If missing items: stage area, prep tools, layout, cleanup |
| **7:00–9:00 AM** | Delivery Owner / Supplier | Material drop, equipment set, unload | Count materials, inspect damage, verify placement | If delivery late: crew does prep, protection, demo, layout |
| **9:00–11:30 AM** | Crew 1 | Primary install / prep work | First section complete, no blockers, area ready for next trade | If blocked: punch list, staging, pre-build, site cleanup |
| **11:30–12:00 PM** | Foreman | Midday check / call inspector / confirm next step | Inspection time locked, next crew ready, no missing material | If inspection slips: hold crew on prep, protection, or cleanup |
| **12:00–3:00 PM** | Crew 2 / Crew 1 | Follow-on work that depends on morning completion | Prior work signed off / ready, area clear, no rework needed | If delayed: backup task list, offload staging, demo, drilling, protection |
| **3:00–4:30 PM** | Foreman + Crew Leads | Cleanup, restock, prep for Day 2 | Site safe, materials staged, tomorrow’s first task ready | If work still blocked: shift to punch, protection, housekeeping |
| **4:30–5:00 PM** | Foreman | End-of-day review | Confirm Day 2 delivery, inspection, access, crew start time | Escalate any missing items now |

**Risks / Hold Points:**  
______________________________________________________________  
______________________________________________________________

---

## DAY 2 — PRODUCTION / INSPECTION DAY

| Time Block | Crew / Owner | Work to Run | Check Before Next Crew Moves | Backup Work if Delayed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **6:30–7:00 AM** | Foreman + Leads | Morning plan, confirm inspector/delivery ETA | All crews know hold points and sequence | If plan shifts: reassign backup tasks immediately |
| **7:00–9:00 AM** | Crew 1 | Complete prerequisite work for inspection | Area complete, clean, accessible, ready for inspection | If held up: prep, corrections, staging, protection |
| **9:00–10:30 AM** | Inspector / Foreman | Inspection window | Signoff received before covering up or moving on | If inspector late: keep crew on standby work |
| **10:30–12:00 PM** | Crew 2 | Next-phase work after signoff | Inspection passed, no open corrections | If failed: fix list, recheck, no downstream work |
| **12:00–2:30 PM** | Delivery Owner / Crew 1 | Deliveries tied to next phase | Materials verified and staged before install starts | If delivery late: use float task list, prep, cleanup |
| **2:30–4:30 PM** | Crew 3 / Crew 2 | Production / install / closeout work | Prior phase complete, no clashes with other trades | If blocked: punch, staging, protection, offload |
| **4:30–5:00 PM** | Foreman | End-of-day reset | Tomorrow’s first task clear, issues flagged, materials confirmed | Escalate unresolved items tonight |

**Risks / Hold Points:**  
______________________________________________________________  
______________________________________________________________

---

## DAY 3 — CLOSEOUT / RECOVERY / FLOAT DAY

| Time Block | Crew / Owner | Work to Run | Check Before Next Crew Moves | Backup Work if Delayed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **6:30–7:00 AM** | Foreman + Leads | Review open items, confirm what’s still waiting | List of blockers, open inspections, missing deliveries | Reorder priorities based on what’s actually available |
| **7:00–10:00 AM** | Crew 1 | Finish open work / corrections / final install | All prior punch items cleared | If blocked: site cleanup, prep next area, material handling |
| **10:00–11:30 AM** | Inspector / Foreman | Final inspection or re-inspection window | Signoff in hand before demob or cover-up | If delayed: keep crew on punch / corrections |
| **11:30–2:30 PM** | Crew 2 / Crew 3 | Backup work / float work | Use only if primary task is waiting | Punch list, protection, staging, demo, cleanup, rework |
| **2:30–4:00 PM** | Foreman + Leads | Final cleanup, demob prep, next-job staging | Site safe, tools counted, materials secured | If still open: assign one crew to finish, one to tidy |
| **4:00–5:00 PM** | Foreman | Closeout call / next-week handoff | Open issues logged, next steps assigned | Escalate unresolved items with owner + ETA |

**Risks / Hold Points:**  
______________________________________________________________  
______________________________________________________________

---

# Foreman Hold Point Checklist
Use this before any crew moves to the next step:

- [ ] Delivery arrived and was checked
- [ ] Materials counted and undamaged
- [ ] Area is clear and ready
- [ ] Prior crew finished and cleaned up
- [ ] Inspection passed or was rescheduled
- [ ] Access is still open
- [ ] Tools / equipment are on site
- [ ] Backup work is assigned if blocked

---

# Backup Work Menu
If a delivery or inspection slips, send the crew here instead of letting them wait:

- Site cleanup / debris removal
- Staging materials
- Layout / marking / measuring
- Pre-assembly / pre-cutting
- Protection / covering finished work
- Punch list corrections
- Tool/equipment check and prep
- Demo / strip-out / haul-off
- Restocking / organizing laydown area

---

# Daily Foreman Notes
**What slipped today?**  
______________________________________________________________

**What is the next hold point?**  
______________________________________________________________

**Who needs a call tonight?**  
______________________________________________________________

**Tomorrow’s first move:**  
______________________________________________________________

---

If you want, I can turn this into a **clean one-page fillable form** or a **job-specific version** for trades like framing, concrete, drywall, or MEP.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Tight enough to run with, and it reads like somebody who’s actually on a site, not in an office. The hold points and backup work are the part that matters — keeps the crew from standing around when something slips.

I can use that template.
